glitches.name: Backwards Long Jump (BLJ)
glitches.label: BLJ_label
glitches.description: A glitch where Mario repeatedly performs backwards long jumps against an obstacle or slope, allowing him to zoom backwards at an incredibly high speed.

variants.name: LBLJ
variants.label: LBLJ_label
variants.description: A glitch where Mario repeatedly performs backwards long jumps against the pillars in the lobby of Peach's Castle, allowing him to clip through a star door while moving at a high speed.

variants.name: SBLJ (Side BLJ)
variants.label: SBLJ_label
variants.description: A glitch performed on the stairs in Peach's Castle where Mario repeatedly performs backwards long jumps sideways against the stairs to gain a high speed and bypass a star door.

applications.name: DDD Skip
applications.label: DDD_skip_label
applications.description: A glitch that applies the Side BLJ technique on the stairs in Peach's Castle—repeatedly performing backwards long jumps sideways against the stairs—to move at a high speed and bypass both a star door and the water wall at the entrance of Dire, Dire Docks simultaneously.

variants.name: BLJ Strat (Musharna)
variants.label: BLJ_strat_label
variants.description: A glitch performed on a rising elevator in Hazy Maze Cave, where Mario repeatedly performs backwards long jumps to clip through the wire fence and reach the star location.

glitches.name: Miscellaneous Wall Clipping
glitches.label: Miscellaneous_wall_clipping_label
glitches.description: A glitch that allows Mario to clip through walls without performing any special actions.

glitches.name: Ledge Clipping
glitches.label: Ledge_clipping_label
glitches.description: A technique where Mario grabs onto or pulls himself up from a specific part of a ledge, causing his body to clip through the wall.

applications.name: Cannonless
applications.label: Cannonless_label
applications.description: A precise setup in Whomp's Fortress where Mario performs a ledge grab animation to clip into a wall and directly grab a hidden star.

glitches.name: Ship Clip
glitches.label: Ship_clip_label
glitches.description: A wall clipping technique in Jolly Roger Bay where Mario swims at full speed and presses the button at a precise time to clip directly through the sunken ship's wall to enter it.

glitches.name: Box TJ
glitches.label: Box_TJ_label
glitches.description: A glitch performed in Hazy Maze Cave where Mario performs a Triple Jump on top of a box, such as a '!' block, to pass upwards through the ceiling and move to another area.

glitches.name: Ground Pound Clipping
glitches.label: Ground_pound_clipping_label
glitches.description: A glitch that exploits the intended mechanic where Mario momentarily stays in place in mid-air right before initiating a downward ground pound, allowing him to pass downwards through moving platforms or objects like Whomps in Whomp's Fortress.

glitches.name: Bob-omb Clip
glitches.label: Bob-omb_clip_label
glitches.description: A glitch that forces Mario through walls or doors, such as the Chain Chomp's gate in Bob-omb Battlefield, by using the knockback from a Bob-omb's explosion or the backward force generated when throwing a bloated Bob-omb while standing against a wall.

applications.name: Pillarless
applications.label: Pillarless_label
applications.description: An advanced glitch in Shifting Sand Land that uses the significant backward momentum from holding and throwing a bloated Bob-omb to clip into the top entrance of the pyramid.

glitches.name: Chuckya Clip
glitches.label: Chuckya_clip_label
glitches.description: A glitch that forces Mario through a wall by having a Chuckya grab Mario, or by using the backward force generated when Mario throws a Chuckya while having his back against a wall. This can be used in courses like Tall, Tall Mountain or Wet-Dry World.

glitches.name: MIPS Clip
glitches.label: MIPS_clip_label
glitches.description: A glitch performed in the basement of Peach's Castle where Mario holds MIPS the rabbit and walks into the corner of a wall or door; upon letting go, Mario is pushed through to the other side, such as a star door.

glitches.name: Door Clip
glitches.label: Door_clip_label
glitches.description: A glitch used in Big Boo's Haunt where Mario is frozen in place when a text box from a Boo appears, allowing him to bypass the door's opening animation and pass through it.

glitches.name: Monkey Bug
glitches.label: Monkey_bug_label
glitches.description: A glitch in Tall, Tall Mountain where Mario grabs Ukkiki the monkey, moves near the star cage, and forces him to drop by performing a jump and ground pound, which drastically cuts short the monkey's intended walking animation.

glitches.name: Pole Glitch
glitches.label: Pole_glitch_label
glitches.description: A glitch performed in Bowser in the Fire Sea, where grabbing the base of a pole instantly teleports Mario upward to the wire grate above.

glitches.name: Insta Clip
glitches.label: Insta_clip_label
glitches.description: A glitch in Tall, Tall Mountain where Mario lands in a lower body of water and uses the recoil to simultaneously touch an upper body of water, teleporting him instantly to the surface of the upper water.
